candidate_check.description: RHEL 8 must automatically lock an account until the locked account is released by an administrator when three unsuccessful logon attempts occur during a 15-minute time period.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file is configured to lock an account until released by an administrator after three unsuccessful logon attempts:

$ sudo grep 'unlock_time =' /etc/security/faillock.conf

unlock_time = 0

If the "unlock_time" option is not set to "0", or is missing or commented out, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure the operating system to lock an account until released by an administrator when three unsuccessful logon attempts occur in 15 minutes.

Add/Modify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file to match the following line:

unlock_time = 0
title: RHEL 8 must automatically lock an account until the locked account is released by an administrator when three unsuccessful logon attempts occur during a 15-minute time period.
